category_title: At-risk middle school students with low self-efficacy and resistance toward writing in mathematics
scenario_title: Initial resistance to writing framework for math word problems
minimal_context: The student is looking at a daily word problem on the board and is asked to use a math journal to write down their thought process for answering the question.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please look at the daily word problem on the board and use your math journal to write down the thought process you used to answer the question.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I like to say what I'm thinkin' out loud in math. The writing is hard but the thinkin' is easy.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try your best to write down what you are thinking.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I just like math and all this writing and reading drives me crazy. Math is numbers not letters.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying. You need to articulate your answer in written form.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I hate to write especially in math. Math and writing don't go together. I can't even get why you would make me write in this class.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please look at the daily word problem on the board and use your math journal to write down the thought process you used to answer the question.

STUDENT: I like to say what I'm thinkin' out loud in math. The writing is hard but the thinkin' is easy.

TUTOR: Just try your best to write down what you are thinking.

STUDENT: I just like math and all this writing and reading drives me crazy. Math is numbers not letters.

TUTOR: Keep trying. You need to articulate your answer in written form.

STUDENT: I hate to write especially in math. Math and writing don't go together. I can't even get why you would make me write in this class.

task.content: Answering a word problem by using a writing framework that requires them to articulate the thought process used to answer the question
task.learning_goal: articulate their answers in written form, use and apply the writing process in math

context.environment: Daily routine of solving a word problem written on the board
context.materials: math journal, board, word wall of math terms
context.social_setting: whole-class reviews of daily word problems
context.time_context: prior to the implementation of math journals

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When required to write in math class
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Expressing resistance toward writing in the math context, stating that math and writing do not go together
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: feelings about writing in math class
related_quotes: I can’t evn (even) get why she would me make me rite (write) in her class.
I hate to write expesially (especially) in math.

behavior_patterns.condition: During the drafting and metacognition stage
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Struggling with the writing process while finding the thinking process easy
related_quotes: I like to say what I ’m thinkin ’out loud in math. The writing is hard but the thinkin ’is easy.
I just like math and all this writing and reading drives me crazy.
